text: The surgeon performs a 1.5 cm transverse incision below the tip of the twelfth rib, accessing the retroperitoneal space through blunt and sharp dissection of the abdominal wall. A small cavity is digitally prepared for the insertion of a 5 mm trocar laterally beneath the eleventh rib, and a 10 mm trocar medially below the twelfth rib. A blunt trocar with an inflatable balloon and adjustable sleeve is introduced into the initial incision site and secured.
